mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce real yields, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as investors seek duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Real sales (RSAFS at 444,491M) combined with housing starts (HOUST at 1,017K) signal robust consumer demand. Strong demand pressures inflation expectations and forces the Fed to maintain or raise rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in sustained monetary tightness.
regime_hint: late-cycle demand expansion with inflation risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points within 20 days despite both RSAFS and HOUST meeting trigger thresholds, or joint signal produces negative Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 5.8% combined with payroll employment at 139,680K indicates a tight labor market. Tight labor conditions drive wage inflation, which forces the Federal Reserve to maintain higher rates to combat price pressures, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage-price spiral risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite both labor indicators meeting trigger conditions, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 105.07 (index 2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. Rising industrial production without corresponding demand growth signals deflationary supply expansion, which allows the Fed to cut rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 10 basis points over 5 trading days when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 237.633 (index 1982-1984=100) reflects cumulative inflation. If inflation remains elevated relative to the Fed's 2% target, the central bank will maintain restrictive policy, keeping 10-year Treasury yields elevated. This mechanism activates when inflation expectations exceed the Fed's implicit tolerance band.
regime_hint: above-target inflation regime requiring monetary restraint
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points over 20 trading days despite CPI year-over-year growth exceeding 3.0%, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 16,150.6B chained 2017 dollars) combined with housing starts (HOUST at 1,017K) indicates demand-side momentum. Accelerating real demand growth forces the Fed to raise rates to prevent overheating, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST > 950K
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 30 basis points over 60 trading days despite both GDP and housing meeting trigger thresholds, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 5.8% is near structural estimates. If unemployment rises above 6.5% over the next 20 days, it signals labor market deterioration that prompts Fed rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors anticipate monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points within 20 trading days
regime_hint: labor market softening triggering Fed accommodation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite UNRATE increasing > 0.4pp, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 105.07) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS at 139,680K) reveals supply-side constraints. If industrial production declines while employment remains elevated, it signals stagflationary pressure that forces the Fed to maintain high rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.3% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 100K
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite supply contraction and employment strength meeting trigger conditions, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.
